content.text: is the integration of Model Context Protocol into Anthropic's
(Python and TypeScript), allowing developers to connect Claude-powered agents to external tools, databases, and APIs programmatically without building a separate MCP client.
What MCP Enables in the Agent SDK

attrs.alt: Diagram for MCP in the Agent SDK

content.text:
With MCP, your agent can:
| Capability | Example |
|------------|---------|
|

| "How many users signed up last week? Break it down by day" (PostgreSQL) |
|

| List GitHub issues, post to Slack, read from Notion, check Sentry errors |
|

| Query documentation servers, browse filesystems, read API responses |
|
Quickstart: Connect to an MCP Server
The agent connects to the documentation server, searches for information about hooks, and returns results.
Define tools directly in your application:

from claude_agent_sdk import create_sdk_mcp_server

def greet_user(name: str) -> str:
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

server = create_sdk_mcp_server(
    name="my-tools",
    version="1.0.0",
    tools=[greet_user]
)

options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
    mcp_servers={"tools": server},
    allowed_tools=["mcp__tools__greet"]
)
Eliminates need for separate server processes.

options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
    allowed_tools=[
        "mcp__github__*",        # All tools from github server
        "mcp__db__query",        # Only query tool from db
        "mcp__slack__send_message"  # Only send_message from slack
    ]
)

auto-approves MCP but disables ALL safety prompts
- Wildcards grant
syntax expands environment variables at runtime.
SDK doesn't handle OAuth flows automatically, but you can pass access tokens:
When you have many MCP tools, definitions consume significant context.
(enabled by default) withholds tool definitions and loads only needed tools per turn.

- Missing environment variables
- Server not installed (for

commands)
- Invalid connection strings (databases)
- Network issues (remote HTTP/SSE)
Claude automatically discovers schema, writes SQL, and returns results.
SDK MCP vs. MCP Connector API

when you need:
- Local stdio servers
- MCP resources or prompts
- SDK MCP (in-process custom tools)
- More connection control

when you need:
- Remote HTTP servers only
- Tools only (no resources/prompts)
- Messages API integration without SDK
